analysis_description: TTE from round3description (hist day 1/2/3 -> 8/7/6d mapped to CSV day 0/1/2). Implied vol via BS bisection on ATM-near voucher vs VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid; vega at that IV. Neighbor mid gaps m_i - m_{i+1} pooled stats and spread width in samples.
used_to_decide: Confirms IV is numerically stable on tapes; neighbor gaps have positive mean (decreasing mids with strike) with dispersion usable for z-score MR; vega scales with TTE.

analysis_description: Parameter sweep vs v0 (grid on one axis at a time in code): raised Z_THRESHOLD 1.8→2.35, ALPHA 0.02→0.03, smaller BASE_SIZE/MAX_PAIR, TRADE_EVERY=3, added day_idx from timestamp rollback for TTE_days=8-day_idx (per round3description CSV mapping), BS delta hedge on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT. Backtest: v0 total -57,517 vs v1 total -7,850 (same prosperity4bt command, worse fills).
used_to_decide: v1 kept the same neighbor-gap thesis but reduced churn and directional bleed; day-aware TTE and delta hedge align greeks with spec timing; still underwater vs ~800k extract+VEV benchmark so further tuning needed.

analysis_description: Grid search (24 runs) around v1 with IV/greek-aware core unchanged: Z_THRESHOLD in {2.35,2.6,2.9}, TRADE_EVERY in {3,4}, BASE_SIZE in {8,10}, hedge_ratio in {1.0,0.6}. Used same worse-fill round-3 backtest command. Best totals clustered at Z=2.9; top = -908.
used_to_decide: Higher entry threshold is strongly beneficial for this neighbor-gap MR (reduces churn/slippage). Hedging at full delta modestly outperformed 0.6 ratio at the top settings. Promoted simplest tied winner (base_size=8) to trader_v2.

analysis_description: IV second-difference along strike (smile curvature proxy) and gap second-difference stats on ROUND_3 tapes; see analysis_smile_spline_gaps.json. Method: BS IV (r=0) at T=TTE/365 with TTE_days=8-csv_day. For v3 design: centered gap residual R_i=g_i-0.5(g_{i-1}+g_{i+1}) with separate EWMA z_R; micro-grid grid_v3_spline_filter.json varied Z_R_MIN and MAX_SPREAD_SUM — all tied at -908 total vs v2 in this run.
used_to_decide: Simpler v2 logic is the performance driver; spline residual as a same-sign gate does not change total PnL on days 0–2 here (likely same trade set). Kept Z_R_MIN=0.45 and cap=100 for interpretability; IV curvature stats document smile is not flat in second diff.

analysis_description: Theta parameter sweep on v4 (grid_theta_ref_sweep.json): aggressive theta-based size cuts (low THETA_REF) worsened total PnL to -1248 vs v2/v3 -908; neutral ref recovers -908. New tape sample: Pearson correlation of VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid vs mean BS IV across 10 strikes (subsampling timestamps) in analysis_iv_u_mean_corr.json — weak positive ~0.16 on n=116 pairs (method in analyze_iv_u_corr.py).
used_to_decide: Theta as a shrinker is not helpful at strong levels on this backtest; document and set THETA_REF high enough to be inert. IV level co-moves weakly with extract on subsample—does not replace neighbor-gap signal but supports using extract for IV/Greeks.

analysis_description: Pooled half-spread stats by VEV (book) and BS call gamma at strike nearest extract mid with IV from mids (see analysis_vega_gamma_spread.json). Method: TTE from round3 description mapping, r=0. Documents typical spread friction vs gamma scale for the vertical sleeve.
used_to_decide: v5 ranks edges by |z| per two-leg spread; backtest PnL ties best prior (-908) so the ranking change does not improve aggregate on this run—still grounded in book observables for the two-leg cost thesis.

analysis_description: Net-gamma scaling test on selected adjacent vertical: compute BS gamma for both legs at implied vols, use |gamma_hi-gamma_lo| as convexity proxy in clip multiplier. Parameter sweep in grid_gamma_ref_sweep.json for GAMMA_REF in {0.0004, 0.0008, 0.0012, 0.002, 0.0035, 0.006}.
used_to_decide: All tested gamma refs produce identical total -908 on this historical run, indicating current fills/trade set are insensitive to this convexity dampener at these levels; kept simpler default while preserving documented Greek thread.

analysis_description: Book microstructure: L1 microprice vs simple mid for VEV_5000 (subsampled timestamps, all three day files) in analysis_mid_vs_microprice_vev5000.json. Mean micro-minus-mid ~ -0.06 with std ~1.09 — material enough to change gap z-scores when used as quote for VEVs.
used_to_decide: trader_v7 uses microprice for VEV legs only; backtest total -1081 vs v2 -908 (worse). Treat as failed experiment: signal should stay consistent with executable mid for these tapes; retain v2-style mid for vouchers going forward.

analysis_description: Family10 cutoff analysis first: extrinsic quantiles q25=0.5, q50=5.5. WLS quadratic smile RMSE comparison from analysis_family10_extrinsic_cutoff.json: cutoff 0.5 gives lowest IV RMSE (~0.023) while 1.5 reduces price RMSE but uses fewer points. Backtest cutoff comparison (grid_f10_cutoff_backtest.json) under worse fills: cutoff 0.5 total -18,116; 1.5 total -22,265. Chose 0.5 then execution retune grid (grid_f10_exec_tune.json) to stricter OPEN_Z / lower frequency / smaller clip, improving to -3,352.
used_to_decide: Data justified testing both 0.5 and 1.5 cutoffs; 0.5 retained more informative points and performed better in backtest. Execution tuning logically reduced overtrading but family10 variant remains far from target and below neighbor-vertical family.

analysis_description: Duplicate-check driven adjustment against parent index-10 branch. Parent already explored: single-cutoff quadratic WLS smile, theta/spread gates, and microprice blends. To avoid material overlap, switched to dual-cutoff agreement model (0.5 and 1.5) requiring residual sign consensus before trading. Backtest under worse fills improves vs v8 (-2690 vs -3352) but remains far from target.
used_to_decide: Duplicate risk for v8 relative to parent was high due to overlap in cutoff + threshold logic within same family. Dual-cutoff consensus is a distinct assumption and materially different from parent's single-smile gating path.

analysis_description: Deep underlying propagation analysis (analysis_u_move_propagation.json): strike-wise dOption/dU betas decline monotonically from ~0.75 (K4000) to ~0.05 (K5500), with weak spread-shock effect ratio near 1.0 after |dU|>=4. Incorporated into family10 by capping hedge sensitivity with empirical beta and tuning HEDGE_MULT + OPEN_Z (grid_v10_betahedge_tune.json).
used_to_decide: Observed option response elasticity by strike is materially below BS delta for traded wings/low strikes in tape. Scaling hedge by empirical beta (and lowering hedge multiplier) removed large extract hedge drag and improved family10 from -2690 to -599 under worse fills.

analysis_description: Lead/lag and spread-shock work (analysis_lead_lag_spread_ex.json) confirmed synchronous underlying-to-option comovement; no predictive 1-tick lag. Tried parent-style smile axis m=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) for the dual WLS fits. A separate touch-cross entry (|mid-fair|/spread, vega floor, fast cadence) was tested: massive turnover and VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT losses (~-15k per day) vs ~0 in v10, so that path was dropped. Reverted to v10 execution (EWMA z, OPEN_Z/CLOSE_Z, TRADE_EVERY=5) with m-axis fits only. Backtest ties v10 at -599 (days 0-2, --match-trades worse).
used_to_decide: Standardized m improves smile interpretability; on this historical tape it does not beat log(K/S) in PnL when the rest of the stack matches v10. Friction/touch gating without z/EWMA is unsafe here due to hedge churn.

analysis_description: Pooled L1 mean half-spread by VEV and tape OLS beta (dOption/dU) on ROUND_3 days 0-2: beta is highest at VEV_4000 but mean half-spread is ~10.4 vs ~1.4 at VEV_5200; opportunity proxy beta/mean_h peaks ~0.30 at VEV_5200, ~0.27 at VEV_5100, vs ~0.07 at VEV_4000. Script analyze_spread_elasticity_r3.py writes analysis_spread_elasticity_r3.json.
used_to_decide: v12 replaces pure max-|z| pick with |z|*beta/mean_half_spread so wide-spread high-beta symbols do not dominate; backtest (worse) total -138 vs v11 -599 for days 0-2+empty day3 on merged main.
